title: Senior Repair Engineer (Ship / Marine / Vessel)
description:
Responsibilities:

  • Plan, monitor, and track all maintenance works to ensure ships’ operational readiness.
  • Manage and track MRO (Maintenance, Repair & Overhaul) expenditures, including spares and material utilization.
  • Act as the main point of contact for direct customers (ship, SREC, SSEC), maintaining strong relationships and clear communication.
  • Lead and manage a team of technicians, engineers, and contractors to deliver high-quality, safe, and timely work.
  • Conduct Vessel Safety Committee Coordination (VSCC) meetings during ship programs.
  • Prepare and submit monthly management reports in a timely manner.
  • Perform service acceptance for jobs completed by the Production Department.
  • Review, check, and approve Work Orders raised by the Commercial Department.

Requirements:

  • Diploma/Degree in Marine Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Naval Architecture, or Electrical Engineering.
  • Minimum 3 years’ experience in newbuild or ship repair projects preferred.
  • Completion of the Ship Repair Manager Course is advantageous.
  • Strong project management skills in planning, scheduling, costing, risk management, manpower management, and customer relationship management.
  • Effective communicator with the ability to engage and work well with people at all levels.
  • Familiarity with SAP software will be an advantage.
  • Willingness to work onboard vessels and on weekends as required by project needs.

Interested candidates who wish to apply for the advertised position, please click on the APPLY button below to send in your resume.


EA License No: 13C6305


Reg. No.: R22104949 KOH SHI MIN, JOAN (XU SHIMIN)


For candidates who applied for the advertised position is deemed to have consented to us that we may collect, use or disclose your personal information for purpose in connection with the services provided by us.
